title: neotinea ustulata subsp. aestivalis
intro: Neotinea ustulata subsp. aestivalis, also known as the autumnal burned orchid, is a rare subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of certain compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Neotinea ustulata subsp. aestivalis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its possible use in treating various infections. Herbal preparations of Neotinea ustulata subsp. aestivalis are typically made by infusing the plant's aerial parts in hot water or oil, and can be used in the form of teas, tinctures, or topical applications.
